text:
Admission Date:  2001-05-06              Discharge Date:   2017-08-29

Date of Birth:  2001-09-28             Sex:   M

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Martha
Chief Complaint:
s/p Motorcycle crash
Left shoulder and right foot pain

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
2001-05-06 Washout and debridment of left shoulder and right foot
wounds
1998-07-08 I & D of right 1st MPJ
VAC placement


History of Present Illness:
36 yo male driver s/p motorcycle crash travelling ~40mph,
wearing no helmet with +LOC. He was taken to an area hospital
where found to have multiple injuries and was then transferred
to Alameda Co Med Ctr - Fairmont Campus for further care.

Past Medical History:
Denies

Family History:
Noncontributory

Physical Exam:
Upon admission:
BP 126/77  HR 111   RR 14  rom air sats 94%
GCS 15
HEENT: left subgleal abrasion; PERRLA
Back: multiple abrasions on left side, open wound left shoulder
Chest: BS equal, CTA bilat
Pelvis: stable
Rectum: good tone
Extr: open wound right foot; bone exposed
Skin: mulitple abrasions, road rash over abdominal region, left
calvicular region with puncture type wound

Pertinent Results:
2001-05-06 09:06PM   GLUCOSE-130* LACTATE-2.3* NA+-139 K+-4.5
CL--106 TCO2-25
2001-05-06 09:00PM   UREA N-14 CREAT-1.1
2001-05-06 09:00PM   AMYLASE-32
2001-05-06 09:00PM   ASA-NEG ETHANOL-NEG ACETMNPHN-NEG
bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG tricyclic-NEG
2001-05-06 09:00PM   WBC-17.6* RBC-5.18 HGB-17.5 HCT-48.7 MCV-94
MCH-33.7* MCHC-35.9* RDW-13.0
2001-05-06 09:00PM   PLT COUNT-238
2001-05-06 09:00PM   PT-12.5 PTT-23.5 INR(PT)-1.1

CHEST (PA & LAT)  2001-05-06 9:21 PM

FINDINGS: PA and lateral chest radiographs are reviewed without
comparison. Cardiac, mediastinal, and hilar contours are normal.
There is fracture of the left eighth rib laterally, with a small
amount of subcutaneous gas seen in the overlying soft tissues.
There is a small ill-defined airspace opacity in the left lung
base, which could represent evolving contusion. Otherwise, lungs
are clear. There is no pleural effusion. Small left apical
pneumothorax is noted.

Additionally, there is distraction and elevation of the left
acromioclavicular joint, and widening at the acromioclavicular
and coracoclavicular distances, without definite fracture.

IMPRESSION:
1. Small left apical pneumothorax.
2. Left eighth rib fracture, and adjacent ill-defined
parenchymal opacity, which could represent evolving contusion.
3. Grade III left acromioclavicular dislocation.

SHOULDER (AP, NEUTRAL & AXILLARY) TRAUMA LEFT  2001-05-06 9:19 PM

FINDINGS: Three views of the left shoulder are reviewed and
compared to chest radiographs from same day. Grade III
separation of the left acromioclavicular joint is better
appreciated on chest radiographs, due to improved AP
positioning. There is no fracture. Glenohumeral joint is intact.
Large laceration is seen in the supraclavicular soft tissues.
There is no abnormal soft tissue calcification or radiopaque
foreign object.

IMPRESSION:
1. Grade III left acromioclavicular dislocation.
2. Large supraclavicular laceration.


Brief Hospital Course:
He was admitted to the Trauma Service. Orthopedics and Podiatry
were consulted given his injuries. He was taken to the operating
room by Orthopedics for washout and debridement of left shoulder
and right foot wounds. Podiatry took patient to the operating
room several days later for repair of the right 1st MPJ and
placement of VAC. He was discharged to home with plans for
continuing the VAC dressing.

Because of his extensive injuries he did initially have pain
control issues. He received PCA and IV narcotics at first and
then was changed to long acting narcotics (MS Contin) and
Oxycodone for breakthrough pain. A bowel regimen was initiated
as well.

A wound care nursing consult was also placed because of the
multiple abrasions over his back, arms and legs.

Social work was consulted to provide emotional support for
patient and family as a result of this traumatic event.

Physical therapy was consulted and he was eventually cleared for
discharge to home. He was given instructions for follow up with
Orthopedics and Podiatry.

Medications on Admission:
None

Discharge Medications:
1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
2. Bisacodyl 5 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: Two (2)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily) as needed for
constipation.
3. Magnesium Hydroxide 400 mg/5 mL Suspension Sig: Thirty (30)
ML PO Q6H (every 6 hours) as needed for constipation.
4. Acetaminophen 500 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO Q 8H
(Every 8 Hours) as needed for pain.
5. Morphine 30 mg Tablet Sustained Release Sig: One (1) Tablet
Sustained Release PO Q12H (every 12 hours) as needed for pain.
Disp:*60 Tablet Sustained Release(s)* Refills:*0*
6. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q3H (every 3 hours)
as needed for breakthrough pain.
Disp:*90 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
7. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO HS (at bedtime).
8. Albuterol 90 mcg/Actuation Aerosol Sig: Two (2)  Inhalation
every six (6) hours as needed for shortness of breath or
wheezing.


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Us Air Force Hospital 92nd Medical Group

Discharge Diagnosis:
s/p Motorcycle crash
Small left pneumothorax
Left rib fractures 09-21
Left acromioclavicular disclocation
Right renal contusion
Right 1st metatarsal head degloving injury


Discharge Condition:
Good


Discharge Instructions:
It is important that you continue to cough, deep breathe and use
the incentive spirometer 10x every hour while you are awake.

DO NOT bear full weight on your right foot, you may put weight
on the heel portion of your right foot only. Also you should not
put any weight on your left arm because of your shoulder injury.
Wear the sling on your left arm.

Return to the Emergency room if you develop any fevers, chills,
headache, dizziness, chest pain, shortness of breath, productive
cough, abdomial pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea and/or any
other  symptoms that are concerning to you.

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with Wills Eye Hospital Clinic in 2 weeks with Dr. Dickerson,
call 548-12-8289 for an appointment.

Follow up next week in Southwest Regional Medical Center clinic with Dr. Danielle, call
519-297-2845 for an appointment.

Follow up in 2 weeks with Dr. Dickerson, Trauma Surgery, in 2 weeks,
call 211-726-6014 for an appointment.
